Get started

Source: vignettes/GetStarted.Rmd
GetStarted.Rmd

Get started with medusa

medusa is an R package for microsimulation-based distributional analysis of price shocks and poverty assessment. It is built on microdata from the Household Budget Survey (HBS), which collects detailed information on household consumption expenditure and socioeconomic characteristics across EU countries.

The package provides three main modules:

Module Spain EU countries
Distributional impacts calc_di() calc_di_eu()
Energy poverty indices calc_ep() calc_ep_eu()
Transport poverty indices calc_tp() calc_tp_eu()

For a full description of the microsimulation model, see the Distributional impacts article.

Installation

Install the development version from GitHub:

# install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("bc3LC/medusa")

Load the package:

library(medusa)

Spain

medusa includes pre-processed Spanish HBS microdata for 2006–2021. No prior data preparation is needed.

Quick example 

library(medusa)

# Download the example price shocks file
ex_shocks()

# Load the file (after editing the shocks)
shocks <- read.csv("Example_shocks.csv", header = TRUE, sep = ",", dec = ".")

# Calculate distributional impacts for 2019 by income decile
calc_di(2019, shocks = shocks, var_impact = "DECILE")

# Calculate energy poverty indices for 2019
calc_ep(year = 2019, index = "all")

# Calculate transport poverty indices for 2019
calc_tp(year = 2019, index = "all")

For step-by-step guidance, see the Spain tutorials.

EU countries

medusa supports 27 EU member states using Eurostat HBS microdata for the waves 2010, 2015 and 2020. The microdata are confidential and must be requested directly from Eurostat — they are not included in the package.

Workflow 

library(medusa)

# Step 1: Process the raw Eurostat microdata (once per wave)
hbs <- hbs_eu(year = 2015, country = "all", path = "path/to/your/data")

# Step 2: Download the example price shocks file for EU
ex_shocks_eu()

# Load the file (after editing the shocks)
shocks <- read.csv("Example_shocks_eu.csv", header = TRUE, sep = ",", dec = ".")

# Step 3: Calculate distributional impacts
calc_di_eu(data = hbs, shocks = shocks, var = "all")

# Step 4 (optional): Calculate energy poverty indices
calc_ep_eu(data = hbs, index = "all")

# Step 5 (optional): Calculate transport poverty indices
calc_tp_eu(data = hbs, index = "all")

For step-by-step guidance:

  • Preparing the data — how to organise the Eurostat files and run hbs_eu()
  • Calculate distributional impacts
  • Calculate energy poverty indices
  • Calculate transport poverty indices
  • Step-by-step example

Available variables

The socioeconomic and demographic variables available for distributional analysis differ between Spain and EU data. For a full list, see Available Variables.

# Spain
available_var_impact()

# EU
available_var_eu()

Package data

For details on the data sources and processing methodology, see Package Data.

Developed by Eva Alonso-Epelde, Clàudia Rodés-Bachs, María Moyano-Reina, Jon Sampedro, Xaquín García-Muros.
